fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Maryland Pharmacist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a pharmacist? Here's whether your license transfers to Maryland - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: pharmacist license reciprocity maryland, how to transfer pharmacist license to maryland, pharmacist compact maryland, maryland pharmacist endorsement application

board.name: Maryland Board of Pharmacy
board.mailing_address:
Maryland Board of Pharmacy
4201 Patterson Avenue
Baltimore, Maryland 21215-2299

license_types.title: RPh, PharmD
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a pharmacist in Maryland.

steps.title: 1. Create an Account on OneStop
steps.description: Register for an account on the Maryland OneStop portal (onestop.maryland.gov). This is the centralized system for all state applications, renewals, and services.

steps.title: 2. Complete and Submit Online Application
steps.description: Log in to your OneStop account, search for 'Pharmacist', and select the appropriate application type (e.g., Licensure by Examination or Endorsement). Complete all sections of the application and pay the required fees through the portal.

steps.title: 3. Submit Supporting Documentation
steps.description: Upload or arrange for the submission of all required documents as specified in your application, which may include official transcripts from an ACPE-accredited program, proof of internship hours, and criminal background check results.

steps.title: 4. Register for and Pass Examinations
steps.description: For licensure by examination, register with NABP (National Association of Boards of Pharmacy) and pass the North American Pharmacist Licensure Examination (NAPLEX) and the Maryland Multistate Pharmacy Jurisprudence Examination (MPJE). For endorsement, only the Maryland MPJE is required.

steps.title: 5. Await Board Review and Licensure
steps.description: The Maryland Board of Pharmacy will review your complete application, all supporting documents, and examination scores. Once approved, your license will be issued and can be verified online via the Board's verification portal.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NAPLEX and MPJE.

provisional_text: Some states offer an intern or provisional license for graduates actively accumulating required hours or waiting to take the NAPLEX.
reciprocity_text: Pharmacists licensed in another state can often apply via license transfer (reciprocity) through the NABP, though most states still require passing their specific MPJE.

faqs.question: How much does a Maryland pharmacist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies by state. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is the MPJE required in all states?
faqs.answer: Almost all states require passing their state-specific Multistate Pharmacy Jurisprudence Examination (MPJE) to verify knowledge of local pharmacy laws.

faqs.question: Does Maryland have reciprocity for pharmacists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers pharmacist practice in Maryland. To work in Maryland, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my pharmacist license to Maryland?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Maryland while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Pharmacists licensed in another state can often apply via license transfer (reciprocity) through the NABP, though most states still require passing their specific MPJE.

hero.title: Become a Pharmacist in Maryland
hero.dek: To apply for a Pharmacist license in Maryland, you must create an account and submit your application through the official Maryland OneStop portal.

official_links.registration_steps: Create an account on the Maryland OneStop portal by providing your email and personal details, then verify your email. Once logged in, navigate to 'Licenses and Permits' and search for 'Pharmacist' to begin an application.
